prs.title: Planet Comparison Visualization Tool - Shresth
prs.body:
1 : This project is a Planet Comparison Tool that lets users compare planets based on properties like gravity and diameter.
2 : The JavaScript logic maps through the planets data in planets.json file, extracts the selected property, finds the highest and lowest values
3 : Used Chart.js to draw the bar chart, showing each planet’s value.

prs.title: PROJECTS PAGE UPDATE
prs.body:
Update projects page with horizontal slider and improved filter UI

    Changed project layout from vertical list to horizontal slider, displaying one project at a time for better focus and navigation.

    Aligned filter bar alongside the slider for easier access and consistent layout.

    Redesigned filter bar UI to be mostly transparent by default and more visible on hover, enhancing overall aesthetics 

    Verified full functionality of filters and slider interaction in the new layout.

    Made the entire section fully responsive for optimal viewing on all screen sizes.

    Known issue: Unable to fix the “ongoing” filter tab, which currently shows a black screen.


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f7fe234a-be5d-4a65-9649-359a0c2aa135

issues.title: UI Layout Issues on Login and Signup Pages
issues.body: ### Is there an existing issue for this? - [ ] I have searched the existing issues ### Describe the bug Description The Login and Signup pages currently have some UI alignment and spacing inconsistencies, which affect visual balance and user experience. Current Behavior 1 The illustration and form are not aligned consistently. 2 Some elements have uneven spacing and appear visually off-center. 3 The reCAPTCHA section and buttons appear misaligned with the input fields. 4 The layout does not scale smoothly across different screen sizes. Proposed Fix 1 Improve layout using consistent flex/grid alignment. 2 Adjust margins/padding around form fields and text blocks. 3 Ensure responsive breakpoints match the platform’s existing UI standards. 4 Review and align UI with current design guidelines (colors, spacing, typography). I am interested in contributing to this project and would like to work on resolving these UI issues as my first contribution. Kindly assign this issue to me. ### Steps To Reproduce Steps to Reproduce 1 Go to the Login page: https://circuitverse.org/users/sign_in 2 Observe the alignment between the illustration and the login form. 3 Resize the browser window (especially between 768px–1200px width). 4 Notice how the layout becomes unbalanced and certain elements shift or misalign. Similarly: 1 Go to the Signup page: https://circuitverse.org/users/sign_up 2 Observe spacing and alignment around the form fields, reCAPTCHA, and buttons. 3 Resize the browser window. ### Expected Behavior Expected Behavior 1 The content should remain visually balanced on both desktop and smaller screens. 2 Form elements, text, and buttons should have consistent padding and alignment. 3 The illustration and form sections should align in a clean, structured layout. 4 Overall page design should match the style and spacing patterns used across the platform. ### Screenshots Signup Page Login Page ### Device Information [optional] ```markdown - OS:macOS Sequoia - Browser:Google Chrome - version: ``` ### Additional context _No response_ ### Are you working on this issue? Yes

issues.title: [BUG] The astronomy news page has too much padding-top
[BUG] The events page content has too much padding on top
[ENHANCEMENT] Addition of Two Dropdowns: "Explore" and "Community" in Navbar
issues.body: Issue Description: Describe the bug / problem: The current navbar is getting overcrowded as more links are added, causing content overflow on larger screens. To improve usability and maintain a clean layout, we need to restructure the navbar. Proposed changes: Split the existing links into two dropdown menus: Explore: For main site sections or features. Community: For contributors, members, handbook, and related resources.
